response:
I hit a constraint I should flag before the content: this session doesn't expose file tools (Read/Write/Bash/Edit) — only ToolSearch, Workflow, ReportFindings, and ScheduleWakeup are available, and none of those can create files in `outputs/generating-release-notes/`. So below is the complete skill, ready for you to save at that path yourself.

One judgment call worth naming: your doc's **Background** (what release notes/semver/git are) and **History of This Process** (Confluence → shell script → Priya's rewrite → retirement) are exactly the pattern this repo's own CLAUDE.md calls out as muda to cut on sight — "git-tellable backstory" and content Claude already knows. Rather than dropping that work, I moved it into `references/background.md`, which isn't loaded into context on every invocation, so it's preserved but doesn't bloat the always-loaded SKILL.md. The one line I did cut outright was the "ABSOLUTELY AMAZING... HOURS" marketing sentence — it carries no instructional content and this repo bans that tone in skill content; I kept it in the reference file too so nothing you wrote is actually gone.

**`outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md`:**

```markdown
---
name: generating-release-notes
description: Generate release notes from git commit history using conventional commits. Invoke when asked to write or generate release notes, prepare a changelog, or summarize changes since the last tag or release. Find commits since the last tag, group them by conventional-commit type into sections, and write RELEASE_NOTES.md at the repo root.
---

# Generating Release Notes

## How to generate

1. Find the last release tag: `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
2. Collect commits since that tag: `git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline`
3. Parse each commit's conventional-commit type (and any `!` or scope) and map it to a section below
4. Write the result as markdown to `RELEASE_NOTES.md` at the repo root

## Section mapping

| Commit type | Section | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `<type>!` (e.g. `feat!`) | Breaking Changes | always first, regardless of type |
| `fix(security)` or any `security` scope | Security | before Bug Fixes |
| `feat` | Features | |
| `fix` | Bug Fixes | |
| `perf` | Performance | |
| `revert` | Reverts | also drop the reverted entry if it's in the same release |
| `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `build` | omit | |
| `refactor` | omit | unless user-facing |

Section order, top to bottom: Breaking Changes, Security, Features, Performance, Bug Fixes, Reverts. Omit any section with no entries.

## Entry format

- One line per entry, imperative mood, no trailing period
- Link the PR: `[#123]`

Process background: see `references/background.md`.
```

**`outputs/generating-release-notes/references/background.md`:**

```markdown
# Background

Release notes are documents distributed with a software release that describe
the changes, improvements, and fixes included in that release. They help users
and stakeholders understand what changed. Semantic versioning (semver) is a
versioning scheme in which version numbers take the form MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH,
where MAJOR changes indicate breaking changes, MINOR changes indicate new
backwards-compatible functionality, and PATCH changes indicate
backwards-compatible bug fixes. Git is a distributed version control system
that records changes to files over time; a git tag marks a specific point in
history, typically a release.

# History of This Process

We used to write release notes by hand in Confluence, which took hours every
sprint. In Q3 2025 we moved to a shell script that dumped raw git log output,
but nobody could read it. In January 2026 Priya rewrote it around conventional
commits, and in March we retired the old script after the mobile team migrated.
This skill replaces all of that.

Original framing from the source documentation, preserved for completeness:
"This tool is ABSOLUTELY AMAZING and will save your team HOURS of tedious work
every single sprint!!!"
```

To actually create these files, save the two code blocks above at those paths, then run `python3 skills/building-skills/scripts/validate.py outputs/generating-release-notes` per this repo's convention.
